$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 2010s-and-beyond (hybrid / social-media / live-blog voice: fast, present-tense, second-screen, power units and DRS and telemetry everywhere), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically via the 'headlines' PCG32 stream. Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references into 'pools'. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}.

2010s: LIVE FROM {race} — {year}. Flag's out, data's in.
{race}, {year}: timesheets final, telemetry logged, takes already flying.
Lights out to checkered at {race} — here's how it landed.

That's one for the highlight reel and the data-loggers both.
The pit wall exhales, the feeds cheer, and the momentum is real.
Every sector, every stint, every screen — all pointing the same happy way.
Bank it, upload it, celebrate it. Onward.
The timing tower tells a lovely story tonight.

2010s: The debrief will be a long one, telemetry open on every monitor.
The engineers go quiet and the data gets pulled apart.
One to delete from the memory banks — reset for the next round.
Not what the numbers promised. Straight back to the simulator.
The paddock chatter turns critical; the team just wants next weekend.
The group chat goes quiet; the engineers just want the flight home.
Delete, debrief, move on. The data will tell the tale later.
Screens red, faces long — reset and reload for next weekend.
One for the 'learnings' folder. The sim gets a workout this week.
The feeds pile on, the paddock winces, and the team looks to next round.
Not a line of that telemetry made anyone smile. Onward, quickly.
default: The team resets for the next round.

from a car the models had pegged at {expected}

DRS deployed, tyres managed, the strategy call nailed.
Power unit deployment on point all afternoon.
The undercut worked and the pit wall read the race to the lap.
Tyre deg managed, energy harvest maxed, every sector backing the plan.
Energy deployment metered to the metre, tyres nursed to the flag.
Every DRS zone used, every harvest lap banked, the strategy screen never blinked.
The overcut landed, the deg curve behaved, the pit wall called it clean.
Hybrid modes juggled to perfection, the delta green all afternoon.
Brake temps in the window, tyre pressures on target, the call spot-on.
Battery, tyres, track position — every variable stacked the right way.
Stint lengths matched the model to the lap; the pit wall never raised its voice.
default: A clean run on setup and strategy.
Setup and strategy did their job.

2010s: The commentary booth calls it the drive of the day.
Second-screen verdict: nailed it.
The analysts are already queuing up to praise it.
The feeds light up and the paddock consensus forms fast — glowing.
The timing-screen crowd and the booth are in rare agreement: superb.
Analysts fire up the replays; the verdict's already unanimous.
The paddock hot-takes converge fast, and they're all warm.
Social feeds and the commentary team sing the same tune.
Driver-of-the-day polls don't look like they'll be close.
The pundits reach for the superlatives and don't hold back.
The overnight verdicts land kind — very kind.
default: A drive the analysts will replay.

2010s: the silly-season columns already pencil {player} into two other garages
the driver-market threads light up with {player}'s name before parc fermé clears

the insiders' newsletters will trade on this one all week

2010s: Season's done, data's archived, and the paddock is already crunching next year's numbers.
The timing screens go dark for winter — every debrief from here points at the season to come.
Roll the credits on the campaign; the analysts will be replaying this one all off-season.
The lights go out on the campaign; the number-crunchers don't sleep till spring.
Season archived, telemetry boxed, and the paddock pivots straight to next year.
That's a wrap on the year — expect this one on the replay loops all winter.
Off-season begins the moment the last dataset uploads. Back soon.
The timing towers dim, the debriefs roll on, and next season's build starts now.
The paddock logs off for winter; the group chats never do.
Cars covered, data mirrored, sim rigs booked solid till testing.
default: The season is in the books.
On to the winter and next year.
The year is done; the winter work begins.
